summary: Tests pass (115/115) and the branch reduced pre-existing TS errors from 127→23, showing significant progress. However, ~9 newly introduced TS errors remain from incomplete threadId→channelId rename: ActiveProcess/PendingEntry interfaces not updated, runAudit/auditFixLoop missing `usage` parameter, stale channelId references in orchestrator/executor, and Discord event handlers missing type guards. The new channel-service.ts and fork-task.ts are well-implemented. The core rename across API routes, state manager, types, and DB layers is largely correct.

issues.description: PendingEntry interface still has `threadId: string` (line 18) but findWaitingCustomText() at line 115 reads `entry.channelId`. Rename the interface field from `threadId` to `channelId` and update register() (line 48) accordingly.

ActiveProcess interface has `threadId?: string` (line 30) but line 793 reads `active.channelId`. Rename the field to `channelId` in the interface. Also update line 163 which sets `threadId: options.channelId`.
chat() options type has `threadId?: string` (line 54) but handlers.ts:470 passes `channelId`. Rename to `channelId` in the options type definition.

startRefixPipeline calls this.runAudit() with 6 args (line 337) but runAudit() at line 1392 requires 7 (missing `usage: ChatUsageResult`). Same for auditFixLoop at line 343 — called with 8 args but needs 9.
executeTaskInBackground: parameter named `threadId` (line 1050) but function body uses `channelId` at line 1057. Rename parameter to `channelId`.
runAudit: parameter named `threadId` (line 1396) but body uses `channelId` at lines 1403, 1413. Rename parameter to `channelId`.

ChannelUpdate/ChannelDelete event handlers: `newChannel.guild` / `channel.guild` fails TS check because type includes DMChannel which has no `guild` property. Add type guard: check `if (!('guild' in newChannel))` or `if (channel.isDMBased()) return` before accessing `.guild`.
sendChatInternal: line 564 uses `threadId` (undefined) in `mq.delete(threadId, msgId)`. Should be `channelId`. This is a runtime bug — progress messages won't be cleaned up after completion.
makeState() helper: `goalSeq` uses partial type with `number | undefined` but GoalDriveState.goalSeq is non-optional `number`. Add a default value like `goalSeq: 1`.
handleGoalDrivePrompt: line 548 `this.messageQueue.sendLong(threadId, ...)` — threadId is undefined (renamed to channelId at line 512). Pre-existing but still needs fix. Same at lines 625, 665.
syncFromDiscord uses hardcoded fallback cwd '/default' for channels without existing DB records. Consider using guild default working directory.
